page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, it provides 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core running at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This instance includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, while offering zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance is also robust, with Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 2707.87 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 29.86 GB/sec. The hardware excels in text and image processing, as well as Redis SET operations, which achieved 63.56 million operations per second.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation, high-speed local NVMe storage, and advanced vector/matrix instruction support, this server is well-suited for workloads requiring rapid local data access and high-throughput processing. It is highly effective for single-threaded compression tasks, text processing, and medium-scale language model inference, where it achieves strong prompt processing speeds. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of a high-density Intel Xeon processor and integrated local NVMe SSDs provides a balanced resource profile for data-intensive applications, database operations, and localized high-performance computing tasks without the overhead of external storage attachments.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a compute-optimized server configured with 32 vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C x86_64 processor featuring 16 physical cores, a 504 MB L3 cache, and a 2.3 GHz clock speed. The instance includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing, image processing, and single-threaded compression. Multi-core capabilities and Redis SET operations also rank highly, while memory bandwidth remains average. This server is optimized for high-throughput workloads, including database operations, localized data processing, and medium-scale language model inference. It offers a cost-effective balance of dedicated compute power and high-speed local storage for demanding enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd features 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and Intel AMX support for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a 32-vCPU server with 120 GB RAM and 1745 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: A high-throughput compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, advanced matrix extensions, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 120 GB RAM
Includes 1745 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Delivers top-tier single-core performance in text and image processing
Best suited for database operations, data compression, and medium-scale LLM inference
